mechanism_narrative: A positive trailing trend in INDPRO signals improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical earnings, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the production trend remains above its recent baseline.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 104.739 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the 20-day forward return hit rate after positive 3-month INDPRO changes is below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward window or the Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS crossing above 1.0% after a long low-rate period marks a tightening phase that raises discount rates and reduces equity duration support, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.0% after being below 1.0% within the prior 12 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 1.16 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is rising while UNRATE is at or below 4.5%, household income and spending jointly support near-term revenue expectations, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 474841.0 and UNRATE was 4.4
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not outperform the unconditional 20-day return by at least 25 basis points on average in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST break below 1200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and durable-goods spillovers, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 1200 thousand units or remains below 1200 for two consecutive releases
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1155.0 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances show positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the average triggered return exceeds the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in CPIAUCSL combined with a rising FEDFUNDS path indicates inflation pressure forcing tighter policy, compressing valuation multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 245.03 and FEDFUNDS was 1.16
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term has nonnegative coefficient in a walk-forward return regression for two consecutive 3-year windows or triggered 20-day returns exceed baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS trend signals ongoing job creation and lower recession probability, supporting risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the signal is observed.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 146730.0 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS 3-month changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of cases over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in UNRATE signals deterioration in labor-market slack and rising recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised lower.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its trailing 6-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.4 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if this UNRATE trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or has a return spread above zero versus non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with FEDFUNDS below 2.0% identifies a growth-with-accommodation regime in which earnings improve before policy becomes restrictive, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 104.739 and FEDFUNDS was 1.16
regime_hint: early-to-mid tightening with policy still accommodative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal's 60-day forward return is not above the unconditional mean in at least 60% of rolling 3-year walk-forward validation windows.
